resolutions.decision: Which server-side library or tool should generate the PDF invoice file?
resolutions.rationale: DP3 carries an explicit cut tag `deferred:S3`; the foundation cut's `deferred[]` names 'PDF generation library/tool selection — defer_to: S3'. C5 (Invoice Export) owns the concern, and S3 is the earliest slice that builds PDF generation. No skeleton seam exercises PDF generation; the walking skeleton (S1) streams no PDF. INV5 fixes the delivery model (server-side + browser download) but does not force the library choice at skeleton time. Resolving now would be premature-waterfall design of a box-internal HOW (§1.2 / RM9).

resolutions.decision: Which OAuth integration library or mechanism should handle the OAuth handshake, token exchange, and session establishment within the application?
resolutions.rationale: C2 (Identity & Auth) is in-skeleton. FD2 (OAuth provider integration boundary) is needed by S1; the skeleton must complete a full Google OAuth 2.0 round-trip (seam `primary_external_integration`, AC5). S1 cannot be built without a concrete library wiring the redirect, callback, and token exchange routes inside C2. The choice is skeleton-once: every subsequent slice inherits the authenticated session C2 establishes. ADR-0002 fixed Python runtime, eliminating Node.js (Passport.js) and Ruby (OmniAuth) candidates. Among remaining Python options, a framework-native OAuth library (django-allauth or Authlib matching the chosen framework) is preferred over hand-rolled code because no force at A13/INV6 personal-tool scale justifies custom CSRF state and token-validation implementation.

resolutions.decision: How should application secrets and configuration (OAuth credentials, database connection strings, hosting environment values) be injected into the running application?
resolutions.rationale: S1 must load OAuth credentials (A2, ADR-0005) and the PostgreSQL connection string (ADR-0003) at startup before any component can function. This is a cross-cutting structural choice the skeleton must fix to be coherent — every component that reads a secret agrees on the injection mechanism. ADR-0006 fixed deployment to a PaaS platform (Render, Fly.io, Railway) whose native env-var UI provides the injection mechanism without additional infrastructure. A6 (portability, no vendor lock-in) eliminates vendor-tied managed secrets services. Dotenv files committed to the repository are rejected on security grounds traceable to A2. Runtime environment variables + dotenv-for-local-dev (gitignored) is the only option satisfying both A2 and A6 at the scale fixed by A13/INV6.

resolutions.decision: What build and test strategy should be used to mechanically prove each acceptance criterion is met: unit and integration tests, end-to-end browser automation, or a mixed approach?
resolutions.rationale: DP11 concerns the CI/build pipeline and test toolchain — 'inside the build pipeline' per its blast_rationale. No component in `components.json` owns the CI/test infrastructure; it is not an HLD box. The skeleton pass draws application domain components only. DERIVE-TESTS (Phase 3 role 7) is the designated resolver for build/test strategy decisions. Resolving here would be out-of-lane (prompt §8: 'No tests/build-DAG (DERIVE-TESTS)'). Deferred to DERIVE-TESTS pass.

local_adrs.title: Adopt framework-native OAuth library for OAuth handshake within Identity & Auth
Adopt environment variables as secrets and configuration injection strategy
